actions.name: set_away_admin

away_mode_enabled.description: Set to "true" to change the status of the admin to away.

away_mode_reassign.description: Set to "true" to assign any new conversation replies to your default inbox.

away_status_reason_id.description: The unique identifier of the away status reason

actions.risk: high: Set an admin to away; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: create_content_import_source

sync_behavior.description: If you intend to create or update External Pages via the API, this should be set to `api`.

status.description: The status of the content import source.

url.description: The URL of the content import source.

actions.risk: high: Create a content import source; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: delete_content_import_source
actions.risk: high: Delete a content import source; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: update_content_import_source

sync_behavior.description: If you intend to create or update External Pages via the API, this should be set to `api`. You can not change the value to or from api.

status.description: The status of the content import source.

url.description: The URL of the content import source. This may only be different from the existing value if the sync behavior is API.

actions.risk: high: Update a content import source; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: create_external_page

title.description: The title of the external page.

html.description: The body of the external page in HTML.

url.description: The URL of the external page. This will be used by Fin to link end users to the page it based its answer on. When a URL is not present, Fin will not reference the source.

ai_agent_availability.description: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by AI Agent. Will not default when updating an existing external page.

ai_copilot_availability.description: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by AI Copilot. Will not default when updating an existing external page.

source_id.description: The unique identifier for the source of the external page which was given by Intercom. Every external page must be associated with a Content Import Source which represents the place it comes from and from which it inherits a default audience (configured in the UI). For a new source, make a POST requ

external_id.description: The identifier for the external page which was given by the source. Must be unique for the source.

actions.risk: medium: Create an external page (or update an external page by external ID); live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: delete_external_page
actions.risk: high: Delete an external page; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: update_external_page

title.description: The title of the external page.

html.description: The body of the external page in HTML.

url.description: The URL of the external page. This will be used by Fin to link end users to the page it based its answer on.

fin_availability.description: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by Fin.

source_id.description: The unique identifier for the source of the external page which was given by Intercom. Every external page must be associated with a Content Import Source which represents the place it comes from and from which it inherits a default audience (configured in the UI). For a new source, make a POST requ

external_id.description: The identifier for the external page which was given by the source. Must be unique for the source.

actions.risk: medium: Update an external page; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: create_article

title.description: The title of the article.For multilingual articles, this will be the title of the default language's content.

description.description: The description of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the description of the default language's content.

body.description: The content of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the body of the default language's content.

author_id.description: The id of the author of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the id of the author of the default language's content. Must be a teammate on the help center's workspace.

state.description: Whether the article will be `published` or will be a `draft`. Defaults to draft. For multilingual articles, this will be the state of the default language's content.

parent_id.description: The id of the article's parent collection or section. An article without this field stands alone.

parent_type.description: The type of parent, which can either be a `collection` or `section`.

translated_content.description: The Translated Content of an Article. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the article.

description.description: The description of the article.

author_id.description: The ID of the author of the article.

state.description: Whether the article is `published` or is a `draft` .

created_at.description: The time when the article was created (seconds).

updated_at.description: The time when the article was last updated (seconds).

state.description: Whether the article will be `published` or will be a `draft`. Defaults to draft. For multilingual articles, this will be the state of the default language's content.

actions.name: delete_article
actions.risk: high: Delete an article; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: post_export_reporting_data_enqueue
actions.risk: medium: Enqueue a new reporting data export job; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: start_fin_conversation

conversation_id.description: The ID of the conversation that is calling Fin via this API.

message.description: A message exchanged within a Fin Agent conversation.

author.description: The author that created the message.

body.description:
The body of the message. Accepts both plain text and HTML format.
When sending a message to Fin, this should contain the user's message.
Fin's response will be returned as HTML.

timestamp.description:
The timestamp when the message was created.
Used to deduplicate messages sent within a 5 minute window.
Ideally should include milliseconds for higher precision.

timestamp_ms.description:
The timestamp when the message was created, with millisecond precision.
Only present in webhook event responses (fin_replied).

user.description: A user object representing the user in a Fin Agent conversation.

id.description:
The ID of the user. This value will be used to uniquely identify the user
during a conversation with Fin. Maps to the user_id field on the Intercom User object.

name.description: The name of the user.

attributes.description:
A hash of attributes associated with the user.
Attributes can be used by Fin to target content and responses.
Limit to 10 attributes.

attachments.description: An array of attachments to include with the message. Maximum of 10 attachments.

items.description:
An attachment object representing a file or URL attachment included with a message.
Attachments can be used to provide additional context to Fin.
Maximum of 10 attachments per request.

url.description: The URL of the attachment. Required when type is 'url'. Must be publicly accessible.

name.description: The name of the file. Required when type is 'file'.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the file. Required when type is 'file'.

data.description: Base64-encoded file data. Required when type is 'file'.

conversation_metadata.description: Metadata about the conversation, including history and attributes.

history.description:
An array of previous messages in the conversation before Fin is initialized.
This data provides context to Fin and helps generate a better answer.
Limit to the last 10 messages.

items.description: A message exchanged within a Fin Agent conversation.

author.description: The author that created the message.

body.description:
The body of the message. Accepts both plain text and HTML format.
When sending a message to Fin, this should contain the user's message.
Fin's response will be returned as HTML.

timestamp.description:
The timestamp when the message was created.
Used to deduplicate messages sent within a 5 minute window.
Ideally should include milliseconds for higher precision.

timestamp_ms.description:
The timestamp when the message was created, with millisecond precision.
Only present in webhook event responses (fin_replied).

attributes.description:
A hash of attributes associated with the conversation.
These attributes can be used by Fin to provide more contextual responses.
Limit to 10 attributes.

actions.risk: medium: Start a conversation with Fin; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: reply_to_fin

message.description: A message exchanged within a Fin Agent conversation.

author.description: The author that created the message.

body.description:
The body of the message. Accepts both plain text and HTML format.
When sending a message to Fin, this should contain the user's message.
Fin's response will be returned as HTML.

timestamp.description:
The timestamp when the message was created.
Used to deduplicate messages sent within a 5 minute window.
Ideally should include milliseconds for higher precision.

timestamp_ms.description:
The timestamp when the message was created, with millisecond precision.
Only present in webhook event responses (fin_replied).

user.description: A user object representing the user in a Fin Agent conversation.

id.description:
The ID of the user. This value will be used to uniquely identify the user
during a conversation with Fin. Maps to the user_id field on the Intercom User object.

name.description: The name of the user.

attributes.description:
A hash of attributes associated with the user.
Attributes can be used by Fin to target content and responses.
Limit to 10 attributes.

attachments.description: An array of attachments to include with the message. Maximum of 10 attachments.

items.description:
An attachment object representing a file or URL attachment included with a message.
Attachments can be used to provide additional context to Fin.
Maximum of 10 attachments per request.

url.description: The URL of the attachment. Required when type is 'url'. Must be publicly accessible.

name.description: The name of the file. Required when type is 'file'.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the file. Required when type is 'file'.

data.description: Base64-encoded file data. Required when type is 'file'.

company_id.description: The company id you have defined for the company. Can't be updated

plan.description: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

size.description: The number of employees in this company.

website.description: The URL for this company's website. Please note that the value specified here is not validated. Accepts any string.

industry.description: The industry that this company operates in.

custom_attributes.description: A hash of key/value pairs containing any other data about the company you want Intercom to store.

remote_created_at.description: The time the company was created by you.

monthly_spend.description: How much revenue the company generates for your business. Note that this will truncate floats. i.e. it only allow for whole integers, 155.98 will be truncated to 155. Note that this has an upper limit of 2**31-1 or 2147483647..

count.description: The number of times the event occurred.

last.description: The last time the event was sent

created_at.description: The time the message was created. If not provided, the current time will be used.

actions.risk: medium: Create a message; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: create_news_item

title.description: The title of the news item.

body.description: The news item body, which may contain HTML.

sender_id.description: The id of the sender of the news item. Must be a teammate on the workspace.

state.description: News items will not be visible to your users in the assigned newsfeeds until they are set live.

deliver_silently.description: When set to `true`, the news item will appear in the messenger newsfeed without showing a notification badge.

labels.description: Label names displayed to users to categorize the news item.

reactions.description: Ordered list of emoji reactions to the news item. When empty, reactions are disabled.

newsfeed_assignments.description: A list of newsfeed_assignments to assign to the specified newsfeed.

items.description: Assigns a news item to a newsfeed.

newsfeed_id.description: The unique identifier for the newsfeed which is given by Intercom. Publish dates cannot be in the future, to schedule news items use the dedicated feature in app (see this article).

published_at.description: Publish date of the news item on the newsfeed, use this field if you want to set a publish date in the past (e.g. when importing existing news items). On write, this field will be ignored if the news item state is "draft".

actions.risk: medium: Create a news item; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

visible_to_contacts.description: Whether the attribute is visible to contacts when creating a ticket in Messenger.

allow_multiple_values.description: Whether the attribute allows multiple files to be attached to it (only applicable to file attributes)

actions.name: create_ticket

ticket_type_id.description: The ID of the type of ticket you want to create

contacts.description: The list of contacts (users or leads) affected by this ticket. Currently only one is allowed

id.description: The identifier for the contact as given by Intercom.

conversation_to_link_id.description:
The ID of the conversation you want to link to the ticket. Here are the valid ways of linking two tickets:
 - conversation | back-office ticket
 - customer tickets | non-shared back-office ticket
 - conversation | tracker ticket
 - customer ticket | tracker ticket

company_id.description: The ID of the company that the ticket is associated with. The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom

created_at.description: The time the ticket was created. If not provided, the current time will be used.

ticket_attributes.description: The attributes set on the ticket. When setting the default title and description attributes, the attribute keys that should be used are `_default_title_` and `_default_description_`. When setting ticket type attributes of the list attribute type, the key should be the attribute name and the value of

admin_assignee_id.description: The ID of the admin to which the ticket is assigned. If not provided, the ticket will be unassigned.

team_assignee_id.description: The ID of the team to which the ticket is assigned. If not provided, the ticket will be unassigned.

actions.risk: medium: Create a ticket; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute

actions.name: update_ticket

ticket_attributes.description: The attributes set on the ticket.

ticket_state_id.description: The ID of the ticket state associated with the ticket type.

company_id.description: The ID of the company that the ticket is associated with. The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom. Set to nil to remove company.

open.description: Specify if a ticket is open. Set to false to close a ticket. Closing a ticket will also unsnooze it.

is_shared.description: Specify whether the ticket is visible to users.

snoozed_until.description: The time you want the ticket to reopen.

admin_id.description: The ID of the admin performing ticket update. Needed for workflows execution and attributing actions to specific admins.

assignee_id.description: The ID of the admin or team to which the ticket is assigned. Set this 0 to unassign it.
